digest.component_or_area: release pipeline build/publish gating + Hatchling provenance hook
propositions: The published wheel is built from the extracted sdist (no .git present) so it bakes COMMIT = None while only the sdist retains the real SHA.
The build job uploads dist/ with no install or functional test before publish, so the released artifact is never exercised until post-publication when PyPI immutability forces yank+republish.
The only test asserting a non-null baked SHA builds a WheelBuilder directly against the checkout, bypassing the failing sdist-to-wheel path so the defect is uncaught.
Adopt a build-once pattern: build wheel+sdist once, test the exact artifacts in a clean job, and publish the same bytes after verifying recorded SHA-256 hashes without rebuilding.
Fix the hook to read REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT (a short SHA) as primary source, fall back to git rev-parse --short HEAD when unset, and fail the build only when the env var is set but unresolvable.
Prove env-var propagation with a test that runs REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT python -m build and asserts the resulting wheel carries that exact short SHA.
